prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
```pddl
(create_case_for_batch distribution_unit_1)
(assign_investigator_to_case distribution_unit_1 investigator_resource_1)
(schedule_laboratory_test distribution_unit_1 analytical_test_1)
(mark_supply_node_indicator_active distribution_unit_1 supply_node_1 analytical_test_1)
(record_test_result_and_mark_case_ready distribution_unit_1 analytical_test_1)
(assign_quality_representative_to_case distribution_unit_1 quality_representative_1)
(confirm_distribution_unit_containment distribution_unit_1 supply_node_1 quality_representative_1)
(unassign_quality_representative_from_case distribution_unit_1 quality_representative_1)
(release_laboratory_test_resource distribution_unit_1 analytical_test_1)
(create_case_for_batch manufacturing_unit_1)
(assign_investigator_to_case manufacturing_unit_1 investigator_resource_2)
(schedule_laboratory_test manufacturing_unit_1 analytical_test_1)
(record_test_result_and_mark_case_ready manufacturing_unit_1 analytical_test_1)
(attach_mitigation_to_mfg_unit manufacturing_unit_1 contamination_indicator_1 mitigation_resource_1)
(create_case_dossier_with_mitigation manufacturing_unit_1 distribution_unit_1 contamination_indicator_1 supply_node_1 case_dossier_1)
(apply_mitigation_and_update_mfg_unit manufacturing_unit_1 contamination_indicator_1 analytical_test_1 mitigation_resource_1)
(validate_case_dossier case_dossier_1 manufacturing_unit_1 analytical_test_1)
(propagate_release_to_distribution_unit distribution_unit_1 case_dossier_1)
(propagate_release_to_mfg_unit manufacturing_unit_1 case_dossier_1)
(release_laboratory_test_resource manufacturing_unit_1 analytical_test_1)
(create_case_for_batch decision_record_1)
(assign_investigator_to_case decision_record_1 investigator_resource_3)
(schedule_laboratory_test decision_record_1 analytical_test_1)
(record_test_result_and_mark_case_ready decision_record_1 analytical_test_1)
(assign_quality_representative_to_case decision_record_1 quality_representative_1)
(assign_executive_approver_to_decision decision_record_1 executive_approver_1)
(notify_clinical_representative_and_record_notification decision_record_1 clinical_representative_1 analytical_test_1)
(acknowledge_clinical_notification decision_record_1 quality_representative_1)
(unassign_quality_representative_from_case decision_record_1 quality_representative_1)
(record_external_approval decision_record_1 executive_approver_1)
(release_executive_approver_from_decision decision_record_1 executive_approver_1)
(finalize_external_approval_and_lock decision_record_1)
(record_final_batch_release_and_mitigation decision_record_1 shortage_mitigation_resource_1 analytical_test_1)
(apply_final_release_to_decision_record decision_record_1 investigator_resource_3 shortage_mitigation_resource_1)
(release_laboratory_test_resource decision_record_1 analytical_test_1)
(create_case_for_batch decision_record_2)
(assign_investigator_to_case decision_record_2 investigator_resource_3)
(schedule_laboratory_test decision_record_2 analytical_test_1)
(record_test_result_and_mark_case_ready decision_record_2 analytical_test_1)
(accept_sample_into_dossier decision_record_2 sample_record_2 case_dossier_1)
(attach_third_party_certificate decision_record_2 third_party_certificate_2)
(integrate_third_party_certificate decision_record_2 sample_record_2 case_dossier_1 analytical_test_1 third_party_certificate_2)
(assign_technical_specialist_to_decision decision_record_2 technical_specialist_1)
(assign_quality_representative_to_case decision_record_2 quality_representative_1)
(assign_executive_approver_to_decision decision_record_2 executive_approver_1)
(conduct_technical_specialist_review decision_record_2 technical_specialist_1 quality_representative_1 sample_record_2 case_dossier_1)
(record_executive_approval_and_attach_guidance decision_record_2 executive_approver_1 sample_record_2 case_dossier_1)
(attach_regulatory_guidance decision_record_2 regulatory_guideline_1)
(perform_final_checks_and_mark_ready decision_record_2 manufacturing_unit_1 distribution_unit_1 analytical_test_1 regulatory_guideline_1)
(lock_decision_and_authorize_release decision_record_2)
(record_final_batch_release_and_mitigation decision_record_2 shortage_mitigation_resource_1 analytical_test_1)
(apply_final_release_to_decision_record decision_record_2 investigator_resource_3 shortage_mitigation_resource_1)
(release_laboratory_test_resource decision_record_2 analytical_test_1)
(schedule_laboratory_test distribution_unit_1 analytical_test_1)
(record_final_batch_release_and_mitigation distribution_unit_1 shortage_mitigation_resource_1 analytical_test_1)
(apply_final_release_to_distribution_unit distribution_unit_1 investigator_resource_1 shortage_mitigation_resource_1)
(schedule_laboratory_test manufacturing_unit_1 analytical_test_1)
(record_final_batch_release_and_mitigation manufacturing_unit_1 shortage_mitigation_resource_1 analytical_test_1)
(apply_final_release_to_mfg_unit manufacturing_unit_1 investigator_resource_2 shortage_mitigation_resource_1)
```
message.reasoning:
The problem is a planning problem in PDDL for pharmaceutics batch quarantine and release. I need to output only the sequence of ground actions that solve it, each on its own line in the form (action-name arg1 arg2 ...).


## Understanding the Problem  
- The task focuses on solving a planning problem in PDDL for pharmaceutics batch quarantine and release.  
- The goal is to output a sequence of ground actions, each on a new line in the format (action-name arg1 arg2 ...).  

## Defining the Output  
- Only the sequence of ground actions is required, with no additional explanation or metadata.  
- Each action must follow the specified format, ensuring clarity and machine-readability.

## Examining Initial Conditions  
- Key resources available include case_dossier_1, sample_records_1 and 2, third_party_certs_1 and 2, regs_1 and 2, shortage_mit_1, tech_spec_1, and clinical_reps_1 and 2.  
- Decisions (decision1, decision2) are linked to manufacturing_batch_unit_1 (mfg1), distribution_unit_1 (dist1), dossier1, and respective samples.  

## Analyzing Types and Hierarchy  
- Product_batch_record is a type, with decision_entity_group and potentially manufacturing_batch_unit as subtypes, used interchangeably in predicates.  
- Manufacturing_unit_1, distribution_unit_1, and decision_records can function as product_batch_record where needed, despite initial setup lacking case_record_created.  

## Determining Action Sequence  
- Goal is to achieve release_applied on mfg1, dist1, decision1, and decision2, requiring release_recorded, investigator_assigned, and batch_has_shortage_mitigation_resource for each.  
- Actions like apply_final_release_to_mfg_unit need preconditions met, with shortage_mitigation_resource_1 initially available and reusable after each application.  

## Planning Process Steps  
- Likely need to start by creating a case for the batch, given no initial ready_for_review status, with units (mfg1, dist1) considered the batches.  
- Process involves investigation, tests, QA, mitigation, dossier creation, validation, technical review, executive approval, and final release recording before applying release.

## Planning Process Steps  
- Creating a dossier requires both units to be ready_for_dossier, with specific combinations of activated mitigation and containment statuses.  
- For units with contamination (e.g., mfg1), choose containment paths; for supply issues (e.g., dist1), activate supply indicators using lab tests and confirm containment with QA.  

- Actions like mark_supply_node_indicator_active need the unit ready, a scheduled test, and linked resources, setting the supply as active.  
- Confirming containment (e.g., confirm_distribution_unit_containment) requires QA assignment, active supply, and sets the unit as ready_for_dossier and contained.  

## Sequencing Actions  
- Start by creating a case for each unit, assigning an investigator, and scheduling a laboratory test, consuming lab availability.  
- After scheduling, activate indicators using the test, record test results to mark cases ready_for_review, and release lab resources if needed.  

## Handling Decisions  
- Both decision1 and decision2 share the dossier and units, likely requiring similar processing for release_recorded and other preconditions.  
- Actions may involve clinical representatives, given decision_linked_to_clinical_representative for both decisions, suggesting clinical paths.

## Exploring Decision Paths  
- There are two decisions (dec1 and dec2), each linked to samples 1 and 2, respectively, suggesting parallel processing for dossier integration.  
- Processing a sample for a decision requires it to be ready, linked to a validated dossier, and available, marking it processed and linked to the dossier.  

- After processing, technical review readiness involves a scheduled test and processed sample, setting the decision as tech_review_ready if no manufacturing evidence is in the dossier.  
- A third-party certificate variant requires manufacturing evidence in the dossier, while technical review needs QA and technical assignments, varying by distribution evidence.  

## Evaluating Clinical Paths  
- The clinical notification path involves notifying a clinical representative, requiring the decision ready for review, a scheduled test, and an available representative, consuming availability.  
- For dec1, clinical representative 1 is available initially, but dec2's representative 2 is not, limiting clinical path use to dec1.  

## Considering Alternative Approaches  
- Given clin2’s unavailability, dec2 may need a different path, possibly focusing on technical and executive review after dossier validation.  
- To meet release goals for both decisions, prioritize unit processing, dossier creation, and validation before applying technical paths for decisions.

## Processing Distribution Unit  
- Schedule a laboratory test for distribution_unit_1 with analytical_test_1 and activate supply node indicators.  
- Record test results, mark the case ready, assign and then unassign a quality representative, confirming containment and releasing lab resources.

## Handling Manufacturing Unit  
- Create a case for manufacturing_unit_1, assign investigator_resource_2, and schedule a laboratory test with analytical_test_1.  
- Record test results, mark the case ready, attach mitigation, and ensure readiness for review without needing containment confirmation.

## Creating and Validating Dossier  
- Create a case dossier with mitigation for both units, linking manufacturing evidence, then unassign the quality representative to free them.  
- Apply mitigation, update the manufacturing unit, validate the dossier, and propagate release authorization to both units.

## Managing Decision Processing  
- Attempt to process decision_record_1 using the clinical path, assigning investigator_resource_3 and scheduling a laboratory test.  
- Encounter an issue as the lab is still scheduled for manufacturing_unit_1, blocking further test scheduling.

## Assigning Technical Roles  
- Assigned technical_specialist_1 to decision_record_2, as it's ready for review and the specialist is available, now marked unavailable.  
- Assigned quality_representative_1 to decision_record_2, assuming availability, to prepare for technical review.  

## Conducting Technical Review  
- Conducted technical_specialist_review for decision_record_2, using case_dossier_1 with only manufacturing evidence, completing the review.  
- This path was suitable as the dossier lacks distribution evidence, aligning with review prerequisites.  

## Preparing for Executive Approval  
- Assigned executive_approver_1 to decision_record_2, assuming availability, to handle executive approval.  
- Recorded executive approval and attached guidance for decision_record_2, given it has manufacturing evidence but not distribution evidence.  

## Finalizing Decision Process  
- Attached regulatory_guideline_1 to decision_record_2, as it has executive recording and guidance attached, consuming guideline availability.  
- Planned to perform final checks and mark ready, then lock and authorize release, adjusting due to attached guidance.

## Managing Laboratory Scheduling  
- For decision_record_1, the laboratory test remains scheduled after finalizing results and notifying stakeholders, used in recording final decisions.  
- After applying decision_record_1, the lab is released, freeing resources for other uses.  

## Sequencing Decision Tasks  
- For decision_record_1, assign quality representative and executive approver after marking ready, then notify clinical representatives and acknowledge notifications.  
- Unassign quality representative and executive approver after external approval, finalizing and locking the decision.  

## Handling Decision Record 2  
- Created a case for decision_record_2, assigning investigator_resource_3 and scheduling analytical_test_1, then recorded test results and marked ready.  
- Integrated third-party certificate and conducted technical review, ensuring alignment with manufacturing evidence, preparing for final checks.

## Handling Manufacturing Processes  
- Created a case for manufacturing_unit_1, assigned investigator_resource_2, and scheduled analytical_test_1.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
